rationale.summary: Vote: NO. For this TreasuryWithdrawals (active), downside risk and/or weak safeguards outweighed demonstrated benefit in the current evidence set. Decisive reason: a material claim is unsupported — "The DAO claims it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit. Weighed against it: the proposal substantiates "The Cardano Builder DAO is proposed as an ecosystem-wide, smart contract-governed funding mechanism for projects advancing Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs." and clears the evidence gates. What would change this vote to YES: Provide independent evidence for: The DAO claims it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds; Provide line-item budget. Residual uncertainty: Treasury flow signal is in stressed regime (total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals). Confidence context: score -0.12; confidence 72.0%.
rationale.summary_raw: Advisory model lean was +0.020 (display cap ±0.05) and had zero influence on the binding score: A small positive nudge is justified because the proposal’s stated structure aligns with public-benefit ecosystem growth while including treasury-stewardship features such as independent administration, milestone validation, auditable separate accounts, repayment of unused funds, and cited verifiable references; the nudge remains small because several operational and impact claims are asserted in the proposal rather than evidenced within the anchor text itself.
top_fixes: Provide independent evidence for: The DAO claims it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

sections.title: Intake
sections.conclusion: Baseline fields and source anchors establish whether the proposal can be reviewed at all.

sections.title: Claims and evidence
findings: Proposal document read by reasoning layer: yes
Requested: Fund the Cardano Builder DAO as a smart contract-governed funding mechanism for Cardano builders aligned with Vision 2030 KPIs.
Recipient: Cardano Builder DAO, with funds initially received and administered by an independent council/multisig composed of dRep DAO members before milestone-based disbursement.

Deliverables: Allocate capital to builders expected to improve monthly active users, monthly on-chain transactions, and TVL., Operate proposal review, feedback coordination, transparent fund distribution, and KPI/result tracking for funded work., Continue KPI dashboard development toward live on-chain Cardano data tracking and recurring KPI reporting standards with dReps., Disburse funds only after milestone completion is validated and confirmed by the administrator.

Claim (governance, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The Cardano Builder DAO is proposed as an ecosystem-wide, smart contract-governed funding mechanism for projects advancing Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs.
Claim (adoption, supported_in_proposal, high materiality): The proposal says funded projects will be required to track monthly active users, TVL, and on-chain transactions.
Claim (economic, proposer_asserted, high materiality): The DAO claims it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal claims Round 1 funded 20 companies, distributed 5,541,335 ADA, included 38 voting members and 27 requesting members, and achieved 83% governance participation.

Claim (governance, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal claims Round 2 funded 14 companies, added 18 new members, and achieved 88% member participation after review, debate, temperature checks, and final voting.

Claim (technical, proposer_asserted, medium materiality): The proposal says the DAO built Version 1 and Version 2 KPI dashboards, moving from self-reported KPI tracking toward live on-chain Cardano data.
missing: Independent evidence for: The DAO claims it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds.
Independent evidence for: The proposal claims Round 1 funded 20 companies, distributed 5,541,335 ADA, included 38 voting members and 27 requesting members, and achieved 83% governance participation.
Independent evidence for: The proposal claims Round 2 funded 14 companies, added 18 new members, and achieved 88% member participation after review, debate, temperature checks, and final voting.
Independent evidence for: The proposal says the DAO built Version 1 and Version 2 KPI dashboards, moving from self-reported KPI tracking toward live on-chain Cardano data.
sections.conclusion: Each claim must map to replayable public evidence; proposer assertions alone are not enough for confidence.

sections.title: Treasury analysis
findings: Six-month treasury flow regime: stressed (basis: total inflow (tau + donations) vs enacted withdrawals)
sections.conclusion: Treasury votes require a higher bar because they consume shared ADA and create precedent.

sections.title: Risk review
sections.conclusion: Risk is not a side note; unmitigated execution or governance risk can dominate an otherwise attractive proposal.

sections.title: Counterargument pass
findings: Strongest YES: the proposal substantiates "The Cardano Builder DAO is proposed as an ecosystem-wide, smart contract-governed funding mechanism for projects advancing Cardano Vision 2030 KPIs." and clears the evidence gates.

Strongest NO: a material claim is unsupported — "The DAO claims it has already distributed 11.1 million ADA across 34 proposals in two funding rounds." — so cost or precedent may outweigh the benefit.
Strongest hold: if claims cannot be tied to replayable evidence, abstaining avoids overclaiming certainty.
sections.conclusion: A defensible rationale must show the best opposing case before it reaches a vote.

sections.title: Synthesis
findings: Final vote must be derived from completed sections above, not from a prose summary.
The public rationale should name the decisive section and the strongest counterargument.
sections.conclusion: The vote is only credible if the assessment tree shows enough work for a skeptical delegator to audit.

actionable_asks: Publish a line-item budget: what is being bought, at what unit cost.
State the cost against the benefit: what does this buy, per ada spent.
Describe the funding path beyond this grant, or say plainly that there isn't one.
Disclose the rate basis — headcount, FTE fraction, or day rate — so the price can be checked against the market.
Tie payment to defined deliverables, not to elapsed time or headcount.
State your AI-cost assumption: this work is cheaper to produce than it was, and the price should show it.
missing: cost comparables, budget proportionality, or sustainability evidence

decision_contract.composite_formula: 40% ecosystem benefit + 25% delivery confidence + 20% cost efficiency + 15% downside protection - 0.50
decision_contract.benefit_formula: 18% critical infrastructure + 8% open source + 15% adoption + 12% ecosystem leverage + 10% strategic necessity + 10% prior delivery + 10% established service + 8% builder dependency + 5% low substitutability + 4% non-funding disruption risk
decision_contract.yes_rule: All quality floors, independently verified evidence, verified NCL, and portfolio capacity must pass.
decision_contract.no_rule: Requires affirmative independently verified waste/harm plus ecosystem benefit below 55%; missing evidence alone is never NO.
decision_contract.portfolio_gate: Verified on-chain Net Change Limit and opportunity-cost portfolio eligibility are mandatory for treasury direction.

flags.detail: No links to forum discussion or community feedback
Treasury withdrawal to a single address

treasury_capacity.caveat: Set by an Info governance action. Info actions change no ledger state and can NEVER be enacted -- they always expire -- so no formally ratified NCL exists or can exist. This is the strongest available public chain evidence: an anchor document byte-verified against its on-chain blake2b-256 hash, covering the current epoch, which won a majority of participating DRep stake. It is NOT enacted ledger state, and the majority may fall short of the supermajority other action types require. Published with every treasury rationale that relies on it.
